prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on a financial model assuming the stock price follows a Geometric Brownian Motion. An estimated annual drift (expected return) of 8% and an annual volatility of 22% were used. The point estimate reflects the projected price after 22 trading days based on this drift. The confidence intervals are calculated using the estimated volatility and standard normal distribution quantiles corresponding to the respective confidence levels. It's important to note that stock price predictions are highly uncertain and rely on simplified assumptions about market behavior.
